# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.where(
      name: params[:search][:name],
      category_id: params[:search][:category_id]
    ).where("price BETWEEN ? AND ?", params[:search][:min_price], params[:search][:max_price])

    # Allowlist column names and operators
    allowed_columns = %w[name category_id price]
    allowed_operators = %w[= >= <= > <]

    @products = @products.where(
      allowed_columns.map do |column|
        next if params[:search][column].blank?

        operator = allowed_operators.find { |op| op == params[:search][column] }
        raise ArgumentError, "Invalid operator" unless operator

        "#{column} #{operator} ?", params[:search][column]
      end.compact
    )
  end
end
